2606.32034.title: QVal: Cheaply Evaluating Dense Supervision Signals for Long-Horizon LLM Agents
2606.32034.paper_summary: LLM agents increasingly act over long horizons, where a single trajectory can contain hundreds or thousands of actions. In these settings, outcome-only rewards provide too sparse guidance, failing to inform the model about the goodness of intermediate actions. Dense supervision methods aim to solve this problem by scoring intermediate steps, from intrinsic confidence to self-distillation and embedding similarities. However, it is common practice to evaluate them by measuring the downstream performance of a training pipeline that integrates them. This is expensive, conflates supervision quality with training engineering confounders, and renders different methodological families requiring distinct training setups incomparable. As a result, dense supervision methods are rarely benchmarked on common ground. We introduce QVal, a training-free testbed for directly evaluating dense supervision signals. Given a state-action pair, QVal measures how well a method's score is Q-aligned: whether it orders actions according to the Q-values of a strong reference-policy. This lets us compare signals before any training run and separate signal quality from other engineering choices. We instantiate QVal as QVal-v1.0, benchmarking 21 dense supervision methods across four diverse environments and seven methodological families, with over 1.2K evaluation experiments across six open-weight model backbones. We find that simple prompting baselines consistently outperform recent dense supervision methods from the literature, and that performance clusters strongly by family. These findings hold across model sizes, environments, and observation modalities. QVal is designed to be easily extensible to new environments and methods, enabling researchers to iterate on dense supervision methods before any training run.
2606.32034.paper_summary_zh: LLM 代理人越來越多地在長期範圍內行動，其中單一軌跡可能包含數百或數千個行動。<lb/>在這些情況下，僅基於結果的獎勵提供的指導過於稀疏，無法告訴模型中間行動的優劣。<lb/>密集監督方法旨在通過對中間步驟進行評分來解決這個問題，從內在信心到自我蒸餾和嵌入相似性。<lb/>然而，通常的做法是通過衡量整合這些方法的訓練流程的下游性能來評估它們。<lb/>這樣的評估成本高昂，將監督質量與訓練工程的混淆因素混合在一起，並使得不同的方法論家族需要不同的訓練設置而無法進行比較。<lb/>因此，密集監督方法很少在共同的基礎上進行基準測試。<lb/>我們引入 QVal，一個無需訓練的測試平台，用於直接評估密集監督信號。<lb/>給定一個狀態-行動對，QVal 測量一種方法的分數與 Q 值的對齊程度：即它是否根據強參考策略的 Q 值對行動進行排序。<lb/>這使我們能夠在任何訓練運行之前比較信號，並將信號質量與其他工程選擇分開。<lb/>我們將 QVal 實例化為 QVal-v1.0，對 21 種密集監督方法在四個不同環境和七個方法論家族中進行基準測試，並在六個開放權重模型骨幹上進行了超過 1.2K 的評估實驗。<lb/>我們發現，簡單的提示基線始終優於文獻中最近的密集監督方法，並且性能在家族中強烈聚類。<lb/>這些發現適用於不同的模型大小、環境和觀察模式。<lb/>QVal 設計為易於擴展到新的環境和方法，使研究人員能夠在任何訓練運行之前對密集監督方法進行迭代。
2606.32034.author: Sergio Hernández-Gutiérrez et.al.
2606.32034.authors: Sergio Hernández-Gutiérrez, Matteo Merler, Ilze Amanda Auzina, Joschka Strüber, Ameya Prabhu, Matthias Bethge
